semanticCore: Inspiration, rapid sign, revealing, or whispering
quranicRelevance: Divine revelation (Wahy) to prophets, natural instinct/inspiration (e.g. to bees, Moses' mother), and whispering of devils ('yoohna').

keyDerivations.meaning: To reveal, inspire, point, or whisper

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَوْحَى إِلَيْهِمْ أَنْ سَبِّحُوا (19:11): He signaled/indicated to them to glorify God.
يُوحِي بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَى بَعْضٍ (6:112): Whispering/suggesting to one another (devils).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of natural insect sounds.
summaryEn: The root denotes quick indication and revelation, representing divine communication to prophets (Wahy), animal instincts, and malicious whispers.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الإشارة السريعة والوحي، ممثلاً الاتصال الإلهي بالأنبياء (وحي)، والغرائز الحيوانية، والوسوسة الشيطانية.
rawText: أَوْحَى إِلَيْهِ 4 He (God) revealed to him; or spake, or made known, to him by revelation === Also, He suggested to him; or put into his mind. (Mughnee voce أَنْ.) الوَحَآءَ الوَحَآءَ [Make thou] haste; or haste to be first, or before, or beforehand: haste; &c. (S, TA.) فِى أَوْحَى مُدَّةٍ In the shortest period: see an ex. in the first paragraph of art. ثقف. وَحِىٌّ Hasty; (K;) quick; (S, Mgh, Msb, K; applied in this sense to death. (S, Mgh, Msb.)
